verses.dev: वायवायाहि दर्शते । इमे सोमा अरंकृताः । तेभिः सूतास आ गहि ॥
verses.eng: Vāyu, come hither to the feast: these Soma juices have been poured; drink of them, for thou art he who drinketh first.
verses.iast: vāyav ā yāhi darśata । ime somā araṃkṛtāḥ । tebhis sūtāsa ā gahi ॥

sandhi.notes: Surface token aligns with multiple underlying words.
Surface token aligns with multiple underlying words.

verses.eng: O Vāyu, singers with their hymns bring thee, the drinker of the Soma, knowing the sacred days.
verses.iast: vāya ukthébhir jaritāras tvām acchā । jaritāraḥ sutasomā aharvidaḥ ॥

sandhi.notes: Both sides of the boundary are consonantal.
Both sides of the boundary are consonantal.
Surface token aligns with multiple underlying words.

verses.dev: वायो तव प्रपृञ्चती धेनाः । जिगाति दाशुषे उरूची सोमपीतये ॥
verses.eng: To thee the cows, O Vāyu, yield their milk, to thee the wide-extending, for the Soma draught.
verses.iast: vāyo tava prapṛñcatī dhenāḥ । jigāti dāśuṣe urūcī somapītaye ॥

verses.dev: इन्द्रवायू इमे सुताः । उप प्रयोभिरा गतम् । इन्दवो वामुशन्ति हि ॥
verses.eng: Indra and Vāyu, these Soma draughts are poured: come with your favours, for the drops long for you.
verses.iast: indravāyū ime sutāḥ । upa prayobhir ā gatam । indavo vām uśanti hi ॥

sandhi.notes: Surface token aligns with multiple underlying words.
Surface token aligns with multiple underlying words.
Surface token aligns with multiple underlying words.

verses.dev: वायविन्द्रश्च चेतथः सुतानाम् । वाजिनीवसू । तावागतमुप द्रवम् ॥

devWords.alignedIASTUnderlying: vāyav indraś ca cetathaḥ sutānām vājinīvasū
devWords.underlyingDevText: वायव इन्द्रश च चेतथः सुतानाम वाजिनीवसू

verses.eng: Vāyu and Indra, ye discern these Soma juices rich in spoil: come unto our pressed libation.
verses.iast: vāyav indraś ca cetathaḥ sutānām । vājinīvasū । tāv ā gatam upa dravam ॥

sandhi.notes: Surface token aligns with multiple underlying words.

verses.dev: वायो इन्द्रश्च सुन्वत आ यतम् । नः प्रयांसि च । सुतं पिबतं हविः ॥
verses.eng: Come, Vāyu and Indra, to the Soma feast; accept our offered gifts, drink of the juice we pour.
verses.iast: vāyo indraś ca sunvata ā yatam । naḥ prayāṃsi ca । sutaṃ pibataṃ haviḥ ॥

sandhi.notes: Both sides of the boundary are consonantal.

verses.dev: मित्रं हुवे पूतदक्षम् । वरुणं च ऋशादसम् । धियं घृताचीं साधन्ता ॥
verses.eng: I call on Mitra, of pure strength, and Varuṇa, destroyer of foes, who perfect holy thought.
verses.iast: mitraṃ huve pūtadakṣam । varuṇaṃ ca ṛśādasam । dhiyaṃ ghṛtācīṃ sādhantā ॥

verses.dev: ऋतेन मित्रावरुणा ऋतावृधा । ऋतस्पृशा । क्रतुम् बृहन्तमाशाथे ॥
verses.eng: By Law, Mitra and Varuṇa, who grow through Law, who touch the Law, have reached their lofty power.
verses.iast: ṛtena mitrāvaruṇā ṛtāvṛdhā । ṛtaspṛśā । kratum bṛhantam āśāthe ॥

sandhi.notes: Both sides of the boundary are consonantal.

verses.dev: कवि नो मित्रावरुणा तुविजाता । उरुक्षया । दक्षं दधाते अपसम् ॥
verses.eng: The sages Mitra, Varuṇa, of mighty birth, wide-ruling, grant us strength that works through sacred law.
verses.iast: kavi no mitrāvaruṇā tuvijātā । urukṣayā । dakṣaṃ dadhāte apasam ॥
